items.headline: City launches amnesty program for unlicensed businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program targeting shopping centers, likely waiving penalties for businesses operating without proper licenses. This could affect local retail compliance and how the city handles informal or overlooked businesses in the area.

items.headline: New economic incentives for retail and small businesses announced
items.summary: The city is introducing incentive programs specifically designed to support retail, small businesses, and startups in Cupertino. This suggests the city is trying to boost local commercial activity and may change what it costs or how easy it is to do business here.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View approved a development agreement for a new mixed-use residential development on East Middlefield Road. This second reading suggests the project is moving forward and will add housing to the area.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city applied for California's Prohousing Incentive Program Round 4 grant funding. This could bring state money to support local housing development projects.

items.headline: City seeking public input on federal housing funding for 2026-27
items.summary: Santa Clara is opening a public comment period on how to spend HUD (federal housing) grant money for the next fiscal year. If you care about affordable housing or community development projects, this is your chance to weigh in on priorities.

items.headline: City holds community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting to discuss a safe parking initiative, likely addressing vehicle dwelling and homelessness. This is a chance for residents to learn about or weigh in on how the city is handling parking and housing for people living in cars.
